4406901559,https://it.linkedin.com/jobs/view/internship-internal-data-analyst-at-fastweb-%2B-vodafone-4406901559?position=5&pageNum=2&refId=cZ4HiDp5kOojW4s7nq3Psg%3D%3D&trackingId=hihnBAr20QJty6%2BwnEotTQ%3D%3D,Internship - Internal Data Analyst,Fastweb + Vodafone,"Milan, Lombardy, Italy","CHI SIAMO

A partire dal 1° gennaio 2026 Fastweb S.p.A. e Vodafone Italia S.p.A., a seguito della fusione per incorporazione deliberata dalle rispettive assemblee degli azionisti, diventano un’unica realtà societaria sotto la denominazione di Fastweb S.p.A.

Il progressivo processo di integrazione tra le due società, avviato sin da gennaio 2025, rappresenta un’opportunità straordinaria per continuare a offrire a famiglie, imprese e pubbliche amministrazioni servizi sempre più avanzati e soluzioni tecnologiche all’avanguardia facendo leva sulle connessioni fisse e mobili in 5G più performanti del Paese, l’IoT, il Cloud e l’Intelligenza Artificiale e mantenendo al centro la qualità e l’affidabilità che da sempre contraddistinguono Fastweb e Vodafone Italia.
Cosa troverai in Fastweb + Vodafone?
I nostri valori: Spark Passion, Aim High, Stand True orientano il nostro lavoro quotidiano. Insieme creiamo un ambiente dove ogni persona può fare la differenza.
We feel close #BELONGING
Creiamo insieme un ambiente di lavoro informale e accogliente, in un clima orientato alla collaborazione e al rispetto.
Mostriamo apertura e curiosità per incoraggiare idee innovative e generare un impatto tangibile per noi e per i nostri clienti.
We are smart #FLEXIBILITY
Il lavoro per obiettivi, lo smartworking, l’autonomia e la fiducia sono principi chiave della nostra cultura organizzativa.
Ci poniamo sfide ambiziose e le affrontiamo insieme con coraggio e responsabilità.
We learn #IMPACT
Coltiviamo il nostro talento, favorendo la crescita e il cambiamento.
Ispiriamo le persone a essere protagoniste del proprio sviluppo, grazie a programmi innovativi e a percorsi professionalizzanti.
We feel good #WELLBEING
Le persone sono la nostra priorità.
C’è grande attenzione al benessere fisico e psicologico, garantendo un ambiente sicuro e positivo e un sano work life balance.
We are unique #INCLUSION
L’inclusione è una scelta che rende l’ambiente creativo, dinamico e ricco di stimoli.
Facciamo tesoro delle differenze che caratterizzano noi, la nostra clientela e il mondo che ci circonda: dall’incontro delle nostre unicità nasce nuovo valore.
We are sustainable #SUSTAINABILITY
Agiamo ogni giorno per un futuro più sostenibile.
Contribuiamo in modo attivo e misurabile al raggiungimento degli obiettivi globali, generando un impatto concreto sul pianeta e sulla società.
We care #CUSTOMER
Conquistare ogni giorno la fiducia dei clienti è la nostra ambizione.
Puntiamo a offrire esperienze di valore e servizi innovativi, grazie a soluzioni di qualità che rispondano alle esigenze e costruiscano un futuro migliore.
DESCRIZIONE DEL RUOLO
Non vedi l’ora di metterti alla prova? Sei una persona curiosa, creativa, precisa e attenta ai dettagli? Candidati per il nostro stage come Internal Audit Data Analyst Intern per il Team Audit.

L’Internal Audit svolge attività indipendente di assurance e consulenza per migliorare efficacia ed efficienza organizzativa. Supporta il raggiungimento degli obiettivi valutando e rafforzando governance, gestione dei rischi e controlli interni. Opera attraverso un piano annuale di audit e l’esecuzione delle verifiche previste sui processi aziendali. Integra data analytics e monitoraggio continuo per trasformare l’audit da retrospettivo a predittivo, accelerando decisioni data‑driven.
COSA IMPARERAI?
Nel Ruolo Di Internal Audit Analyst Intern, Avrai La Possibilità Di Supportare Il Team Di Audit Nello Svolgimento Delle Attività Previste Nel Piano Audit. In Particolare, Imparerai a
Raccogliere, Selezionare, Organizzare e Analizzare i dati attraverso metodologie statistiche con l’obiettivo di costruire modelli di controllo.
Identificazione di pattern, trend, anomalie e indicatori di rischio tramite strumenti di data analytics.
Supporto nella creazione e nell’aggiornamento di rappresentazioni visive (i.e. report e dashboard) per il management e per il team di audit.

Contributo allo sviluppo ed all’automazione di controlli e script di monitoraggio continuo dei processi, per la mitigazione proattiva dei rischi più significativi ed il miglioramento dei processi aziendali.
Sviluppare e perfezionare i sistemi di continuous monitoring, collaborando con il team per l’integrazione di nuovi KPI e alert nei sistemi di continuous auditing.

Preparare report e modelli previsionali per aumentare l'efficacia e l'efficienza dei test sfruttando l'intelligenza artificiale e l'apprendimento automatico, ad esempio attraverso l’utilizzo di tecniche NPL (Natural Processing Language).

Preparazione di presentazioni e materiali per audit review, follow-up e incontri con il management. Supporto alla documentazione ed alla presentazione delle analisi e dei risultati secondo gli standard di audit.
Verificare l’implementazione delle azioni correttive definite a seguito degli Audit e degli Assessment di Compliance.
MUST HAVE
Hai una laurea preferibilmente magistrale o master in discipline Statistiche e/o Matematiche, Ingegneria ad indirizzo informatico, Data Science, Business Analytics e/o Economiche.
Sai utilizzare molto bene il pacchetto Office.
Hai una spiccata propensione per il ragionamento logico, analitico e sintetico.

CHI SIAMO

A partire dal 1° gennaio 2026 Fastweb S.p.A. e Vodafone Italia S.p.A., a seguito della fusione per incorporazione deliberata dalle rispettive assemblee degli azionisti, diventano un’unica realtà societaria sotto la denominazione di Fastweb S.p.A.

Il progressivo processo di integrazione tra le due società, avviato sin da gennaio 2025, rappresenta un’opportunità straordinaria per continuare a offrire a famiglie, imprese e pubbliche amministrazioni servizi sempre più avanzati e soluzioni tecnologiche all’avanguardia facendo leva sulle connessioni fisse e mobili in 5G più performanti del Paese, l’IoT, il Cloud e l’Intelligenza Artificiale e mantenendo al centro la qualità e l’affidabilità che da sempre contraddistinguono Fastweb e Vodafone Italia.

Cosa troverai in Fastweb + Vodafone?

I nostri valori: Spark Passion, Aim High, Stand True orientano il nostro lavoro quotidiano. Insieme creiamo un ambiente dove ogni persona può fare la differenza.

We feel close #BELONGING

Creiamo insieme un ambiente di lavoro informale e accogliente, in un clima orientato alla collaborazione e al rispetto.

Mostriamo apertura e curiosità per incoraggiare idee innovative e generare un impatto tangibile per noi e per i nostri clienti.

We are smart #FLEXIBILITY

Il lavoro per obiettivi, lo smartworking, l’autonomia e la fiducia sono principi chiave della nostra cultura organizzativa.

Ci poniamo sfide ambiziose e le affrontiamo insieme con coraggio e responsabilità.

We learn #IMPACT

Coltiviamo il nostro talento, favorendo la crescita e il cambiamento.

Ispiriamo le persone a essere protagoniste del proprio sviluppo, grazie a programmi innovativi e a percorsi professionalizzanti.

We feel good #WELLBEING

Le persone sono la nostra priorità.

C’è grande attenzione al benessere fisico e psicologico, garantendo un ambiente sicuro e positivo e un sano work life balance.

We are unique #INCLUSION

L’inclusione è una scelta che rende l’ambiente creativo, dinamico e ricco di stimoli.

Facciamo tesoro delle differenze che caratterizzano noi, la nostra clientela e il mondo che ci circonda: dall’incontro delle nostre unicità nasce nuovo valore.

We are sustainable #SUSTAINABILITY

Agiamo ogni giorno per un futuro più sostenibile.

Contribuiamo in modo attivo e misurabile al raggiungimento degli obiettivi globali, generando un impatto concreto sul pianeta e sulla società.

We care #CUSTOMER

Conquistare ogni giorno la fiducia dei clienti è la nostra ambizione.

Puntiamo a offrire esperienze di valore e servizi innovativi, grazie a soluzioni di qualità che rispondano alle esigenze e costruiscano un futuro migliore.

DESCRIZIONE DEL RUOLO

Non vedi l’ora di metterti alla prova? Sei una persona curiosa, creativa, precisa e attenta ai dettagli? Candidati per il nostro stage come Internal Audit Data Analyst Intern per il Team Audit.

L’Internal Audit svolge attività indipendente di assurance e consulenza per migliorare efficacia ed efficienza organizzativa. Supporta il raggiungimento degli obiettivi valutando e rafforzando governance, gestione dei rischi e controlli interni. Opera attraverso un piano annuale di audit e l’esecuzione delle verifiche previste sui processi aziendali. Integra data analytics e monitoraggio continuo per trasformare l’audit da retrospettivo a predittivo, accelerando decisioni data‑driven.

COSA IMPARERAI?

Nel Ruolo Di Internal Audit Analyst Intern, Avrai La Possibilità Di Supportare Il Team Di Audit Nello Svolgimento Delle Attività Previste Nel Piano Audit. In Particolare, Imparerai a

  • Raccogliere, Selezionare, Organizzare e Analizzare i dati attraverso metodologie statistiche con l’obiettivo di costruire modelli di controllo.
  • Identificazione di pattern, trend, anomalie e indicatori di rischio tramite strumenti di data analytics.
  • Supporto nella creazione e nell’aggiornamento di rappresentazioni visive (i.e. report e dashboard) per il management e per il team di audit.
  • Contributo allo sviluppo ed all’automazione di controlli e script di monitoraggio continuo dei processi, per la mitigazione proattiva dei rischi più significativi ed il miglioramento dei processi aziendali.
  • Sviluppare e perfezionare i sistemi di continuous monitoring, collaborando con il team per l’integrazione di nuovi KPI e alert nei sistemi di continuous auditing.
  • Preparare report e modelli previsionali per aumentare l'efficacia e l'efficienza dei test sfruttando l'intelligenza artificiale e l'apprendimento automatico, ad esempio attraverso l’utilizzo di tecniche NPL (Natural Processing Language).
  • Preparazione di presentazioni e materiali per audit review, follow-up e incontri con il management. Supporto alla documentazione ed alla presentazione delle analisi e dei risultati secondo gli standard di audit.
  • Verificare l’implementazione delle azioni correttive definite a seguito degli Audit e degli Assessment di Compliance.

MUST HAVE

  • Hai una laurea preferibilmente magistrale o master in discipline Statistiche e/o Matematiche, Ingegneria ad indirizzo informatico, Data Science, Business Analytics e/o Economiche.
  • Sai utilizzare molto bene il pacchetto Office.
  • Hai una spiccata propensione per il ragionamento logico, analitico e sintetico.
  • Hai abilità comunicative, organizzative e di problem solving.
  • Hai ottime capacità relazionali, con spirito d’iniziativa e proattività.
  • Hai propensione al team working.
  • Hai un’ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese.

NICE TO HAVE

  • Conosci i principali Tool di Data Analytics, di reporting per la presentazione dei dati e di modelli predittivi basati su machine learning.
  • Conosci le principali tecniche di data visualization
  • Conosci i principali database relazionali
  • Conosci i principali linguaggi di programmazione (SQL, Phyton, R, ecc.)
  • Conosci le principali soluzioni di Business Intelligence.

4413327338,https://it.linkedin.com/jobs/view/it-bi-and-integration-specialist-at-etro-4413327338?position=9&pageNum=2&refId=cZ4HiDp5kOojW4s7nq3Psg%3D%3D&trackingId=6cI9pfZGJAX6TH%2Bw7KTQMA%3D%3D,IT BI and Integration Specialist,Etro,"Milan, Lombardy, Italy","L’AZIENDA

Fondata nel 1968 da Gerolamo Etro, oggi Etro è un marchio di lusso con una distribuzione globale e che vanta il riconoscimento da parte dei consumatori di tutto il mondo. Originariamente specializzato nella produzione di tessuti d’arredamento, utilizzando fibre naturali e nobili, Etro ha ampliato la sua offerta nel corso degli anni, diversificando la produzione in abbigliamento prêt-à-porter uomo, donna e bambino, pelletteria, accessori per la casa, profumi e occhiali.

Grazie alla sua unicità e alla combinazione di tradizione artigianale e innovazione estetica, Etro è diventato sinonimo di eleganza senza tempo e di elevata maestria sartoriale: il mix eclettico di motivi, colori e textures, dal paisley alle stampe, è ciò che rende Etro un marchio di lusso italiano unico e rappresentativo.
SCOPO DELLA POSIZIONE

La risorsa entrerà a far parte del team IT con la responsabilità di progettare, implementare e governare l’integrazione tra le principali applicazioni aziendali (ERP, CRM, PLM, WMS, HR, e-commerce e altri sistemi core), assicurando la coerenza dei processi, la qualità del dato e la continuità operativa all’interno dell’application landscape aziendale.
Inoltre, contribuirà a rendere i dati disponibili e fruibili per le piattaforme di Business Intelligence e reporting aziendale.
RESPONSABILITA’
Analizzare i requisiti funzionali provenienti dalle diverse funzioni aziendali (Finance, Supply Chain, HR, Commerciale, Digital)
Progettare e implementare integrazioni tra sistemi core e applicazioni satellite
Gestire interfacce e flussi dati tramite:
API (REST/SOAP)
Web Services (SOAP/WSDL)
Middleware / ESB
Contribuire alla definizione e all’evoluzione dell’architettura di integrazione e gestione del dato
Supportare la costruzione e l’alimentazione dei sistemi di Business Intelligence e Data Warehouse
Collaborare con team BI per garantire qualità e consistenza dei dati, disponibilità e performance dei flussi dati
Collaborare con fornitori esterni e team interni per la corretta configurazione e integrazione delle applicazioni
Supportare le attività di test (UAT) e curare la documentazione tecnica e funzionale
Monitorare le performance delle integrazioni e dei flussi dati
Identificare e proporre interventi di miglioramento su integrazioni, processi e qualità del dato
Assicurare la compliance con standard di sicurezza, governance IT e policy aziendali
PROFILO

Laurea in Informatica, Ingegneria Gestionale o discipline affini
Esperienza su ecosistemi applicativi complessi e integrazione dati

-CRM

-WMS

4414146892,https://it.linkedin.com/jobs/view/it-specialist-at-apple-4414146892?position=10&pageNum=2&refId=cZ4HiDp5kOojW4s7nq3Psg%3D%3D&trackingId=JkG9opRtZg7x%2F2q8M2Pv0g%3D%3D,IT-Specialist,Apple,"Milan, Lombardy, Italy","Riepilogo

Lo store è il luogo che racchiude ciò che di meglio abbiamo da offrire. Mettiamo a disposizione la nostra competenza per aiutare le persone a fare ciò che amano, offrendo un’esperienza unica. Crediamo che l’inclusione sia una responsabilità condivisa: insieme, ci impegniamo a promuovere una cultura capace di accogliere tutte e tutti, in cui ogni persona sia motivata a dare il meglio.

Nel ruolo di Specialist, contribuirai a promuovere la brand loyalty e ad acquisire nuovi clienti attraverso la vendita di prodotti e servizi Apple. Lavorerai con dedizione alle vendite e per creare un’esperienza di assistenza senza precedenti. Il ruolo di Specialist è caratterizzato dalla curiosità: poni domande per scoprire di più sulle esigenze dei clienti, suggerendo loro prodotti su misura capaci di migliorare le loro vite.
In questo ruolo, puoi optare per una posizione full-time, part-time o part-time a tempo determinato.
Descrizione

Offrire un servizio eccellente ai clienti Apple, cercando di capire le loro esigenze, identificando le opportunità di ownership, presentando prodotti e servizi, e informandoli sulle modalità di acquisto pertinenti.
Conoscere e aggiornarsi in modo proattivo su prodotti, servizi, opzioni di acquisto Apple e procedure di vendita della Product Zone.
Supportare i colleghi e le colleghe in tutto lo store in base alle necessità, condividendo le conoscenze su prodotti, servizi e opzioni di acquisto Apple e offrendo opportunità di ownership.
Lavorare con la massima precisione e attenersi alle linee guida.
Svolgere altre attività in base alle necessità, tra cui – ma non solo – fornire assistenza ai clienti e supporto in altre aree dello store.
Contribuire a creare un ambiente inclusivo, promuovendo il rispetto delle differenze e la voglia di imparare.

Lavorerai con dedizione alle vendite e per creare un’esperienza di assistenza senza precedenti. Il ruolo di Specialist è caratterizzato dalla curiosità: poni domande per scoprire di più sulle esigenze dei clienti, suggerendo loro prodotti su misura capaci di migliorare le loro vite.

In questo ruolo, puoi optare per una posizione full-time, part-time o part-time a tempo determinato.

4413421690,https://it.linkedin.com/jobs/view/market-insights-internship-at-coca-cola-hbc-4413421690?position=5&pageNum=5&refId=TKSPfhHrz1KunXbyoLkXMw%3D%3D&trackingId=1uXrSJBZhdmx80gj2OP2Sg%3D%3D,Market Insights Internship,Coca-Cola HBC,"Sesto San Giovanni, Lombardy, Italy","MARKET INSIGHTS INTERNSHIP
UNISCITI AL TEAM
Se ti interessa l’ambito dell’analisi dei dati e vuoi lavorare in un ambiente giovane, dinamico ed inclusivo, questa è l’opportunità per te!

Durante il percorso di stage, apprenderai e approfondirai gli aspetti legati alle attività di analisi del mercato e degli Shopper delle nostre categorie, al fine di fornire all’azienda la conoscenza strategica necessaria per influenzare le decisioni di business.
Nella figura di Market Insights Intern ti interfaccerai con i team di Channel Marketing, Portfolio Development, Data Insights & Analytics e Revenue Growth Management, Category Management.
COSA FAREMO INSIEME
• Identificazione delle opportunità di mercato tramite la comprensione dei trend di vendita e quote di mercato delle nostre categorie
• Preparazione di summary e presentazioni per la funzione Marketing ed Executive Sales (direttori di canale e direzione commerciale)
• Supporto alla costruzione di selling stories fondamentali per la nostra funzione commerciale per la negoziazione sul campo
• Analisi dei dati al fine di supportare l’implmentazione di attività di marketing.
COSA TI OFFRIAMO
Prevediamo un inserimento in stage dalla durata di 6 mesi con possibilità di proroga per ulteriori 6 mesi, oltre a:
• Work-life balance: possibilità di lavoro da remoto e orari flessibili
• Opportunità di crescita e di sviluppo: investiamo nel tuo sviluppo professionale
• Ambiente inclusivo: accogliamo la diversità e valorizziamo l’inclusione
SEI LA PERSONA GIUSTA SE…
• Hai una laurea preferibilmente magistrale in Economia, Statistica o Marketing quantitativo
• Hai un’ottima conoscenza di Excel e in generale del pacchetto Office
• Power BI è considerato un nice to have
• Hai una buona conoscenza della lingua inglese
• Hai delle ottime capacità di analisi, pensiero critico e problem solving
• Hai determinazione, motivazione, curiosità e orientamento al risultato.
DOVE LAVORERAI
Sesto San Giovanni (MI), con possibilità di svolgere attività in smartworking.
SCOPRI CHI SIAMO
Coca-Cola HBC Italia, parte del Gruppo Coca-Cola HBC, è il principale imbottigliatore e distributore di prodotti a marchio The Coca‑Cola Company sul territorio nazionale.

Impieghiamo circa 2.000 dipendenti e operiamo attraverso 6 stabilimenti: 3 dedicati alla produzione di bibite situati a Nogara, Oricola, Marcianise, 2 siti di imbottigliamento di acque minerali, Fonti del Vulture a Rionero in Vulture e Lurisia a Roccaforte Mondovì, e un innovativo polo situato a Gaglianico dedicato alla produzione di bottiglie in plastica riciclata (rPET) destinate all’imbottigliamento dei prodotti dell’azienda.
Ci presentiamo sul mercato come un 24/7 Total Beverage Partner, con un portafoglio prodotti in grado di coprire tutte le occasioni di consumo, dalla colazione fino al dopocena.
Il cliente al centro, più forti insieme, agiamo sostenibilmente, favoriamo la semplificazione: questi sono i valori che caratterizzano Coca-Cola HBC.
LA NOSTRA PROMESSA DI INCLUSIONE

Gli ingredienti segreti di Coca-Cola HBC sono una cultura inclusiva e la valorizzazione della diversità delle nostre persone. Offriamo pari opportunità a tutti, costruendo un ambiente sostenibile in cui ogni individuo contribuisce in modo equo al successo collettivo
Le ricerche sono rivolte ai candidati dell’uno e dell’altro sesso (L. 903/77 e L. 125/91). I candidati sono invitati a leggere sul nostro sito: www.cocacolahellenic.it/Privacystatement/
l’informativa sulla privacy (D. Lgs. 196/2003).
Non ti abbiamo ancora convinto?

NOTE DALLA TUA RECRUITER Caterina: “Se pensi che questa opportunità sia per te, anche se non soddisfi tutti i requisiti, candidati comunque..potresti essere la persona di cui abbiamo bisogno. Cosa aspetti? Corri a candidarti e..let’s open up a moment that refresh us all!”

4417766016,https://it.linkedin.com/jobs/view/ai-engineer-intern-at-nhoa-group-4417766016?position=8&pageNum=5&refId=TKSPfhHrz1KunXbyoLkXMw%3D%3D&trackingId=h4zChPwqFkRSzzL6BOVxuA%3D%3D,AI Engineer Intern,NHOA Group,"Milan, Lombardy, Italy","Company Description

At Atlante we develop EV charging stations to enable the transition towards zero-emission: empowering people to move freely in harmony with the planet. We are building Southern Europe’s largest fast and ultra-fast charging network, powered 100% by renewables. Since our launch in Italy in 2021, we serve EV-drivers at our 900+ stations across Italy, France, Spain, and Portugal.

We believe in progress, leveraging on cutting edge technology to accelerate the electric mobility revolution – especially with energy storage to enhance power from the grid. We are proud of our digital platforms that ensure ease of use and full connectivity.
We are Atlanters: a group of passionate and dedicated people always thriving to deliver a reliable service and enjoyable experience to our customers.
Position
Brief Description Of The Role

As AI Engineer Intern, you will join the Data & AI team within the CTOO group and work alongside our AI Engineer on real projects. You will contribute to the development of AI agents, help build and maintain our knowledge infrastructure, and get hands-on experience with the tools and frameworks shaping the future of applied AI. This is not a simulation
column2: you will work on things that go into production.

column1: Value Creation

Support Atlante's AI initiatives by contributing to agent development, knowledge base management, and tool evaluation. Bring fresh perspective and energy to the team while learning how AI gets deployed in a real business context.
Core Responsibilities
Support the development of AI agents for Operations, Finance, and Customer Service.
Help build and maintain the Knowledge Base used across AI projects.
Test and document AI tools, frameworks, and integrations.
Contribute to conversational interfaces and data access solutions.
Research new AI tools and approaches relevant to Atlante's needs.
Requirements
Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in a STEM field (Computer Science, Statistics, Engineering, Mathematics, Physics or similar).
Basic knowledge of Python.
Interest in Generative AI, LLMs, and their applications.
Curiosity to explore new tools and learn fast.
Proactive attitude, you don't wait to be told what to do.
Experience with agent frameworks, MCP, or Claude Code is a plus but not required.
Other information
Governance & Scope
Area of responsibility: Data Platform & AI Department
Geographic scope: Group-wide, with alignment to HQ
Key interactions: Data & AI team
column2: Digital team

column1: Why Atlante
🚀 Work on real AI projects, not exercises.
🧠 Learn from practitioners building AI solutions in production.
⚡ Fast-paced environment where you can grow quickly.
🌍 International team working on sustainable mobility.
🎯 A company that values what you deliver, not how long you've been doing it.
Location: Milan (hybrid)
If you're eager to learn AI by doing it for real, we'd like to hear from you.

This announcement is addressed to both sexes, in accordance with Law 903/77 and Legislative Decree 198/2006, and to people of all ages and nationalities, in accordance with Legislative Decrees 215/03 and 216/03

4409256806,https://it.linkedin.com/jobs/view/junior-data-analyst-stage-at-sdg-group-italy-4409256806?position=9&pageNum=5&refId=TKSPfhHrz1KunXbyoLkXMw%3D%3D&trackingId=4YONdxag1ezDnxMLm5Bbsw%3D%3D,Junior Data Analyst - Stage,SDG Group Italy,"Milan, Lombardy, Italy","In SDG hai l’opportunità di diventare un esperto di Big Data & Analytics e di entrare a far parte di una delle società più all’avanguardia d’Europa in questo ambito. Potrai partecipare a diversi progetti nazionali e internazionali lavorando in team giovani e dinamici. Vengono effettuate valutazioni periodiche di performance e acquisizione di skills che ti permetteranno di fare velocemente carriera nella nostra realtà. Avrai inoltre modo di costruire il tuo career path personalizzato scegliendo tra diversi percorsi. Partendo da un livello base, attraverso training, coaching, mentoring, e un approccio never-stop-learning diventerai un esperto nella progettazione e sviluppo di practices con le quali far adottare ai nostri clienti un’ottica data driven.
Your Challenges:
Acquisire una conoscenza dei big data & analytics a 360°: data architecture, data management, data visualization e data analysis
Sviluppare hard skills nell’utilizzo delle tecnologie di business intelligence e soft skills per relazionarsi con il cliente e il proprio team di lavoro
Capacità di muoversi e interagire in un ambiente internazionale, rapido e dinamico assumendosi gradualmente maggiori responsabilità e autonomia nei tasks
Requirements:

Technology attitude: SQL, VBA, R, Python, tecnologie di Business Intelligence, Data manipulation, Data Management sono solo il punto di partenza ma non preoccuparti se la tua conoscenza è a un livello base: abbiamo un Learning Center per renderti velocemente un Data Cruncher.

Team player: un buon giocatore di squadra è una persona altruista, dinamica, entusiasta, resiliente e positiva. Sono elementi alla base dei Valori SDG e lavoriamo per far crescere una cultura dei valori a tutti i livelli.
Capacità di elaborare informazioni aziendali complesse e di presentarle con sensibilità agli stakeholder aziendali.
Conoscenza lingua inglese: B1/B2 (verrà testata in sede di colloquio)
Rock your future with SDG:

Academy interne e centri di eccellenza qualificati con possibilità di ottenere certificazioni per i tools più utilizzati nel campo dei big data & analytics: Qlik, PowerBI, Tableau, Microstrategy, SAP Analytics, AWS Redshift, Oracle DB, SQL Server, Google Bigquery, Snowflake etc.

Never stop learning: formazione continua per tutto l’arco della carriera con 80 ore di training iniziale e successiva attività di coaching e mentoring. Valutazioni semestrali del potenziale e della performance.
Ambiente internazionale e giovane: età media inferiore a 30 anni e possibilità di trasferte all’estero.
Smart & Remote working.
SDG Group:

Lavorare in SDG significa entrare a far parte di una delle società di management consulting in più rapida crescita nell’ambito dei Business Analytics, Corporate performance Management e Advanced Business Solutions. Con più di 2000 professionisti in tutto il mondo è stata riconosciuta da Gartner tra le 37 società più all’avanguardia nel settore dei Data & Analytics, l’unica italiana e seconda in Europa. SDG opera in Italia con 4 uffici (Milano, Firenze, Verona e Roma) e dislocamenti in Spagna, Portogallo, Francia, UK, USA e Middle East. Mettiamo la nostra esperienza nel settore degli Analytics a disposizione delle aziende per potenziare le loro strategie di business, facendole diventare organizzazioni data-driven di successo.

Over Exsolvæ

Exsolvæ is een Brussels consultancybureau gespecialiseerd in Data en Artificial Intelligence. Wij begeleiden onze partners bij complexe, vaak unieke uitdagingen via consultance, audits en het ontwikkelen van op maat gemaakte oplossingen. Onze aanpak is bewust holistisch: we laten Data Engineering, Advanced Analytics en AI samenkomen, want wij geloven erin dat daar de échte impact ontstaat.
Bij Exsolvæ noemen we onze consultants Solvers
column2: gepassioneerde professionals die met de nieuwste technologieën werken, blijven leren, kennis delen, en gedreven zijn om duurzame oplossingen met écht resultaat te leveren.

column1: Over de rol

Als Data Analyst speel je een sleutelrol in het omzetten van data naar inzichten die het verschil maken bij strategische beslissingen. Je werkt nauw samen met business- en technische teams om sterke datamodellen te ontwerpen, krachtige dashboards te bouwen, en ervoor te zorgen dat data binnen de organisatie correct, veilig en efficiënt wordt verwerkt.
Wat ga je doen?
Datamodellen ontwerpen, ontwikkelen en optimaliseren die de business en strategie ondersteunen
Power BI dashboards bouwen en uitbouwen die complexe data omzetten naar heldere, bruikbare inzichten
Werken met grote datasets, met aandacht voor performance, schaalbaarheid en snelheid
Data-transformaties optimaliseren via Power Query, DAX en SQL best practices
Databricks inzetten voor data preparation, transformatie en modellering
Werken met data in cloudomgevingen zoals Azure en/of AWS om analyses en rapporteringen op te zetten
Data security (RlS/OLS) implementeren en beheren
column2: de juiste toegang voor de juiste mensen

column1: Kwantitatieve en statistische analyses uitvoeren om inzichten te genereren die onderbouwde beslissingen ondersteunen
Bijdragen aan data-standaarden, governance en best practices binnen de organisatie
Mee bouwen aan datakwaliteit, consistentie en betrouwbaarheid
Wat breng je mee?
4+ jaar ervaring als Data Analyst of in een gelijkwaardige rol
Sterke expertise in Power BI, zowel in dashboard design als in performance optimalisatie
Gevorderde kennis van SQL en DAX, met focus of efficiënte queries en berekeningen
Hands-on ervaring met Databricks
Ervaring in Azure- en/of AWS-omgevingen
Bewezen ervaring met tabular models en high-performance analytics
Solide kennis van datamodellering (star schema, dimensional modeling)
Ervaring met role-based security (RLS/OLS)
Een analytische mindset en een gestructureerde aanpak van problemen
Sterke communicatieve skills

At darwind, we are looking for a Data Consultant who enjoys bringing clarity to complexity and turning raw information into meaningful insights.   • Identify patterns, trends and insights that support business decisions.
  • Collaborate with analysts, IT teams and business stakeholders.
  • Improve data quality, documentation and data governance practices.

Learning and growth

  • Continuous coaching
  • Technical and soft‑skills training

  • Real ownership and influence on the projects you contribute to

Who We Are

NORRIQ is a leading international consulting firm headquartered in Denmark and Belgium, with 350+ consultants driving business transformation through technology. Our Financial Services practice includes 100+ specialists supporting banks, insurers, and financial institutions in navigating regulatory challenges, embracing emerging technologies, and meeting evolving client demands. We prioritize growth, innovation, and excellence, offering abundant opportunities for professional development and collaboration.
The Opportunity

NORRIQ is seeking to grow its Data Analytics and Artificial Intelligence team with additional Data Analyst / Data Visualisation Consultants to improve the business performance of financial institutions. You will help our clients to optimize the value they can get from their internal and external data, through improved business performance monitoring and enhanced reporting quality and readability. Join us to work on impactful projects that shape the future of finance.
Responsibilities
Collecting and interpreting internal and external data about customer behaviour via the digital platforms (Web and mobile) and their digital transactions with the bank.
Translate data insight into actionable recommendations in order to coordinate the business needs around data and improve the bank’s digital and marketing strategy and optimize future campaigns.
Collect, analyze, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and correlations
Develop and maintain complex data models, databases, data warehouses and data marts .
Create data visualizations and reports to communicate insights to stakeholders.
Perform data quality checks and ensure data integrity, develop and implement data governance policies and procedures
Stay up-to-date with industry trends and emerging technologies in data analysis and visualization
Your Profile
Master’s degree (or equivalent work experience) in mathematics or statistics, business intelligence, computer engineering, economy.
1-3 years of experience in data projects, preferably within the financial services industry.
Technical Expertise & Tooling: SQL (mandatory), SAS (nice to have), Python & R (to support our AI team), Reporting & Visualisation tools (Power BI, Tableau, Adobe Analytics, ...),.
ETL Processes: Affinity with ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes, including an understanding of data preparation, cleansing, and integration from multiple sources.
Analytical Skills: Strong problem-solving and analytical abilities to translate complex data into actionable insights.
Business Analysis Skills: Experience in requirements gathering, process design and developing action plans.
AI knowledge: AI knowledge & interest to connect easily with our data scientist and machine learning engineers.
Communication & Collaboration: Excellent communication skills
column2: ability to work well in cross-functional teams and engage effectively with stakeholders.

Collaborative Culture: A supportive, innovative team environment that encourages new ideas and professional development.
Global Exposure: International experience working on cross-border projects that shape the financial services industry.
Why Join Us?

At NORRIQ, we are shaping the future of finance through transformation and innovation. If you're passionate about improving financial processes, driving change, and delivering value to clients, this is the perfect opportunity for you.

4417700541,https://be.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-telenet-group-4417700541?position=10&pageNum=0&refId=KLE0ZoS55zXfqUQ5cxz0iQ%3D%3D&trackingId=RLQng7P%2FexLTzVT2KQkOEA%3D%3D,Data Analyst,Telenet group,"Mechelen, Flemish Region, Belgium","You will join the Technology & Partnership Reporting team. Our team plays a key role in supporting senior management's day-to-day decision-making by delivering timely, accurate, and insightful reporting on technology initiatives and partner performance. We work closely with internal technology teams and external partners to align on reporting priorities, track performance, and surface insights that drive action, helping shape how Telenet delivers on its technology and partnership strategy.
Your tasks as a Data Analyst
You develop curiosity and a deep understanding of your domain's business context and processes.
You understand the business context and processes underlying business requests and reformulate them into clear insights questions.
You collect, query, clean, validate, and manipulate data from different sources to support reporting and analytical needs.
You define, build, and automate reporting dashboards relevant to the technology and partnership topics you are working on.
You develop a proactive partnership with your stakeholders, anticipating their needs and bringing data-driven recommendations to the table.
You perform deep-dive analyses on specific insights requests that lead to actionable recommendations for business and technology colleagues.
You contribute to optimizing reporting processes, tools, and methodologies, including the handling of ad-hoc business requests
You take an active role in the community of data professionals within Telenet, through sharing sessions, inspiration, and coaching.
Our ideal Data Analyst
You have strong analytical skills.
You have a hands-on mentality to really dive into the data and find the answers.
You have a constructive mindset and can collaborate effectively with colleagues from various teams, both from a business and a more analytical background.
You can manage an analytics initiative end to end, including strong communication & presentation skills and stakeholder management.
You have experience in reporting and a solid understanding of performance measurement.
You have solid knowledge of one of the main dashboard tools (Qlik Sense, Power BI, or Tableau)
column2: previous Qlik Sense experience is a strong plus, as it is our primary reporting tool.

column1: You have exceptional knowledge of SQL. Experience with Snowflake is a nice to have.
You have an interest in technology, IT, and operational processes
You have a relevant degree and experience:
Master's degree in a quantitative field (business engineering, TEW, computer science, information management, etc.)
Min. 3 years work experience.
Fluent in English. Knowledge of Dutch or French is a bonus.
Our offer to you
At Telenet group, you get more than just an attractive gross salary. We strongly believe in results and ownership: we give trust but also expect commitment and impact. What you achieve truly matters.
In addition to a thirteenth-month salary and holiday pay, you can count on an extensive package of extra benefits that supports you in your daily life as well as at work.
An overview of our benefits:
Mobility budget: compose your own mobility plan, you can opt for a company car or alternative mobility solutions
Meal vouchers and eco vouchers
Mobile phone, mobile subscription, laptop, and employee discounts
Collective bonus and individual bonus (aligned with your performance)
Hospitalization insurance (including an ambulatory plan) and group insurance
20 days of paid leave and 12 compensatory days off

We value your ongoing development, investing in growth via our internal learning platform, coaching, and on-the-job training. You will have opportunities to keep learning and further strengthen yourself in your role.

We also prefer working closely together. We believe strong teams form by frequent interactions and quick transitions. To facilitate a smooth onboarding and rapid integration for new employees, we expect you to be present in the office at least three days a week during the first six months. On other days, you can work remotely from home.
What happens after you apply?

Do you see a match with our position? Then we look forward to receiving your application. We always get back to you. When there’s a match, we will reach out to you to get acquainted and explain the next steps in our selection process.

4409453561,https://be.linkedin.com/jobs/view/shopper-insights-data-analysis-intern-afh-rtm-at-coca-cola-europacific-partners-4409453561?position=15&pageNum=0&refId=KLE0ZoS55zXfqUQ5cxz0iQ%3D%3D&trackingId=iHmtRdwbagTvMyAOQcv1JQ%3D%3D,Shopper Insights & Data Analysis Intern (AfH RTM),Coca-Cola Europacific Partners,"Anderlecht, Brussels Region, Belgium","Step into a world where data meets real impact.
Join Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP) and bring your analytical mindset, curiosity and business sense to a company that’s always evolving.
About The Internship
The Away From Home (AfH) – RTM team is looking for an enthusiastic intern to join us in Anderlecht from June onwards for a 3 to 4-month internship (extension possible).
During this internship, you will support the team in collecting and analysing shopper and commercial data, directly contributing to the preparation of the strategic plan 2027 (ABP).
You will work on real business challenges, helping the team better understand shopper behaviour and translate insights into strategic recommendations.
What You’ll Do
Collect shopper insights across DI/DC and Cash & Carry channels (sell-out data, interviews, shop visits)
Analyse shopper behaviour in drink centers (promotions, activations, pricing, etc.) using qualitative and quantitative data
Support the development of internal data tools (e.g. spill-over WHS/BWS analysis)
Translate data into actionable insights to support strategic decision-making
Contribute to the preparation of the ABP 2027
Who You Are
Student in Economics, Business, Data or similar field
Strong analytical mindset with good Excel skills
Hands-on, proactive and able to work independently
Structured, detail-oriented and curious
Strong communication skills and positive mindset
Flexible, motivated and eager to learn
Fluent in Dutch, French and English
Why join CCEP?
We join for the brands. We stay for the people.
At CCEP, you’ll be part of a fast-paced and supportive environment where you are encouraged to take ownership, bring ideas and grow.
As an intern, you will have a direct impact on a key strategic project (ABP 2027) and gain hands-on experience in data analysis and commercial strategy.
About CCEP
Coca-Cola Europacific Partners is one of the world’s leading consumer goods companies and the largest Coca-Cola bottler globally.
In Belux, around 2,000 colleagues contribute across production, logistics, sales and headquarters, managing the full value chain locally and delivering excellence every day.
Our recruitment vision
At CCEP, everyone is welcome. We value skills, potential and motivation, regardless of background.
For GDPR reasons, please apply via our official platform.
Questions? InternshipsBelux@ccep.com

4419916319,https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-jd-power-4419916319?position=16&pageNum=0&refId=KLE0ZoS55zXfqUQ5cxz0iQ%3D%3D&trackingId=lpwH1KFCv5IatlMyZx3%2Fag%3D%3D,Data Analyst,JD Power,European Union,"Job Description
The Role:

Our EV Volumes (EVV) Content team is responsible for the research, procurement, collection, analysis and presentation of our EV data and the updates to our vast database. The Analyst is supporting the ongoing collection, maintenance, and quality control of relevant global EV data using our proprietary systems and databases. The Data Analyst works closely with the Data Managers to carry out the activities necessary to deliver the data used by our customer.
Responsibilities

Manage the current sources of data, ensuring the timely receipt of data deliveries. Transform the data into a consistent format, capturing it within our databases in a timely manner to meet publication deadlines. Verify the accuracy of reports and assist in correcting any inaccuracies in the data.
Analyse, understand, communicate and support the resolution of data-related issues as they arise.
Finalize and deliver datasets, ensuring the highest standards of data integrity. Guarantee all publication deadlines are met through quality control and efficient data processing.
Develop and automate data production processes using additional tools. Creating scripts to improve data collection, quality assurance and processing efficiency.
Conduct market research and analysis, on all EV related topics, e.g. worldwide electrification trends, OEM and cell-supplier plans and future models.
Support the documentation & improvement of content processes at EVV.
Cooperate in quality checking the data to ensure the highest integrity and data standards are met, identifying and correcting errors prior to publication.
Support and train new colleagues in EVV data production processes.
Support on projects or assist with other activities, as needed by the Senior Managers or Executive Management team.
What You’ll Be Doing In This Role

We are looking for a candidate with a university degree in Business Administration, Engineering, Economics, Statistics/Mathematics, or an equivalent qualification, or relevant professional experience. The ideal profile will have strong technical capabilities, including advanced Excel skills (formulas, pivot tables, Power Query and macros), advanced Python for data cleaning, automation and scripting, as well as intermediate to advanced knowledge of SQL or database querying. Experience with BI tools such as Tableau at an intermediate level is also required.

A solid understanding of the automotive industry, particularly EV market developments, would be highly advantageous. We are seeking someone with strong analytical and problem-solving skills, a highly numerate mindset, and the ability to interpret complex datasets and translate them into clear, actionable insights. Excellent organisational and time management skills are essential, with the ability to consistently meet tight deadlines.
Fluency in English (written and spoken) is required, along with strong communication skills, with the ability to convey information clearly and in a structured way.
The successful candidate will be detail-oriented, highly accurate, proactive, self-motivated, and driven by a continuous improvement mindset.

From a behavioural perspective, we are looking for an individual who is objective and data-driven, a collaborative team player, demonstrates high integrity and professional standards, and is adaptable and solution-oriented.
Company Mission

Our mission in the market we serve is clear. To power every auto-related decision through proprietary data, advanced analytics, deep industry expertise, and seamless workflows that connect insight to action.
Our Values
We POWER Our Customer's Success
We are Innovative, Collaborative and Grounded in Data
We Make Things Easy
We Get It Done
We Start with Trust & Prove it Everyday

JD Power is committed to employing a diverse workforce. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability.
Should you require accommodations during the recruitment and selection process, please reach out to tarecruitment@jdpa.com.

JD Power does not disclose your personal data to unauthorized third parties. However, as a global corporation consisting of multiple affiliated companies in various countries, JD Power has international sites and JD Power uses resources located throughout the world. JD Power may from time to time also use third parties to act on JD Power’s behalf. You agree to the fact that to the extent necessary your personal data may be transferred and/or disclosed to any company within JD Power group of companies as well as to third parties acting on JD Power’s behalf, including also transfers to servers and databases outside the country where you provided JD Power with your personal data. Such transfers may include for example transfers and/or disclosures outside the European Economic Area and in the United States of America. If you are a California or United Kingdom resident, additional disclosures about the information we collect and how we use that information can be found by clicking here.
To all recruitment agencies: JD Power does not accept unsolicited agency resumes and we are not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

4416486518,https://be.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-functional-analyst-at-nexeo-4416486518?position=18&pageNum=0&refId=KLE0ZoS55zXfqUQ5cxz0iQ%3D%3D&trackingId=IGwwCuEeOGDyvKSCcpPNUw%3D%3D,Data Functional Analyst,Nexeo,Brussels Metropolitan Area,"Data Functional Analyst

About the Role: We are looking for a skilled Functional Data Analyst to join our “Data Team” within the B2B Supply Belgium business platform. This platform serves B2B customers in Belgium and consists of around 180 people, including 120 on the business side and 60 on the IS and Data side. You will work on various data use cases, including Metering Data Management, customer reporting, and Green Energy Reporting.
Key Responsibilities:
Act as a liaison between product owners and the technical Data Team, ensuring a clear understanding of business requirements.
Quickly familiarize yourself with the business domain (Metering Data Management) and analyze business requirements to interpret the specs accurately.
Translate business requirements into functional and technical specifications that guide Data Engineers in building data pipelines.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand business processes and document functional requirements.
Plan and organize functional testing and actively manage business validation processes.
Work with application and data source owners to map out functional data flows, using your technical knowledge and market expertise to ask relevant questions.
Required Skills and Experience:
Must-Have:
Experience in the Utilities sector, specifically with Metering/Allocation data.
Proven experience in Functional Analysis and data modeling.
Proactive, hands-on approach with the ability to work autonomously.
Strong communication and negotiation skills, able to engage with stakeholders effectively.
Experience working in Agile methodologies and familiarity with Agile tools such as Azure DevOps or Jira.
Nice-to-Have:
Knowledge of the Belgian Utilities Market (ATRIAS).
Previous experience with utilities market, metering, and allocation data management.
Familiarity with technical systems like SAP ISU.
Knowledge of data platform concepts.
Soft Skills:
Must-Have:
Strong ability to translate business requirements into clear and effective technical solutions.
Eagerness to work on and understand business logic, especially related to pricing.
Flexible and adaptable, able to support multiple topics as required by the business.
Excellent communication skills, particularly when engaging with diverse stakeholders.
Strong team player with the ability to collaborate effectively with global teams.
Ability to work under pressure, especially during month-end closing periods.
Disciplined in documentation practices and time management.
Ability to meet deadlines and complete tasks within established timeframes.
Education and Experience:
Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree.
5-7 years of experience in data analysis or a similar role.
Language Skills:
Fluent in Dutch or French.
Fluent in English.
If you're passionate about data and eager to make an impact in the Utilities sector, we would love to hear from you!

Data Functional Analyst


About the Role: We are looking for a skilled Functional Data Analyst to join our “Data Team” within the B2B Supply Belgium business platform. This platform serves B2B customers in Belgium and consists of around 180 people, including 120 on the business side and 60 on the IS and Data side. You will work on various data use cases, including Metering Data Management, customer reporting, and Green Energy Reporting.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Act as a liaison between product owners and the technical Data Team, ensuring a clear understanding of business requirements.
  • Quickly familiarize yourself with the business domain (Metering Data Management) and analyze business requirements to interpret the specs accurately.
  • Translate business requirements into functional and technical specifications that guide Data Engineers in building data pipelines.
  • Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand business processes and document functional requirements.
  • Plan and organize functional testing and actively manage business validation processes.
  • Work with application and data source owners to map out functional data flows, using your technical knowledge and market expertise to ask relevant questions.


Required Skills and Experience:

Must-Have:

  • Experience in the Utilities sector, specifically with Metering/Allocation data.
  • Proven experience in Functional Analysis and data modeling.
  • Proactive, hands-on approach with the ability to work autonomously.
  • Strong communication and negotiation skills, able to engage with stakeholders effectively.
  • Experience working in Agile methodologies and familiarity with Agile tools such as Azure DevOps or Jira.

Nice-to-Have:

  • Knowledge of the Belgian Utilities Market (ATRIAS).
  • Previous experience with utilities market, metering, and allocation data management.
  • Familiarity with technical systems like SAP ISU.
  • Knowledge of data platform concepts.


Soft Skills:

Must-Have:

  • Strong ability to translate business requirements into clear and effective technical solutions.
  • Eagerness to work on and understand business logic, especially related to pricing.
  • Flexible and adaptable, able to support multiple topics as required by the business.
  • Excellent communication skills, particularly when engaging with diverse stakeholders.
  • Strong team player with the ability to collaborate effectively with global teams.
  • Ability to work under pressure, especially during month-end closing periods.
  • Disciplined in documentation practices and time management.
  • Ability to meet deadlines and complete tasks within established timeframes.


Education and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree.
  • 5-7 years of experience in data analysis or a similar role.


Language Skills:

  • Fluent in Dutch or French.
  • Fluent in English.


If you're passionate about data and eager to make an impact in the Utilities sector, we would love to hear from you!

Autonomous, detail-oriented, and proactive

What's Offered
The opportunity to join a modern and data-driven organization
A collaborative environment where innovation and analytics are highly valued

4416279731,https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-at-traffic-label-limited-4416279731?position=23&pageNum=0&refId=KLE0ZoS55zXfqUQ5cxz0iQ%3D%3D&trackingId=m8aMkGxu%2FMcxL4WQr12Aog%3D%3D,Senior Data Analyst,Traffic Label Limited,European Economic Area,"Senior Data Analyst (UK/EU job market) / System Analyst (could be on CIS job market)
Department: Data Platform
Reports to: Data Engineering Lead
Employment Type: Full Time
Work Model: Fully Remote
Location: Remote EMEA
About Traffic Label

Traffic Label is a fast-growing, data-driven technology company operating in the iGaming and affiliate marketing space, building scalable products focused on performance, analytics, and automation across global markets.
What we’re building
We’re building a Customer Data Platform: one unified player profile across our group of brands. The platform should help the business:
Improve acquisition. Understand who actually converts, and why others don’
Increase conversion. Replace generic flows with journeys built for the specific player in front of us.
Segment players properly. Who’s a high-value player? Who’s at risk of leaving? Who’s a VIP?
Reactivate before churn. Spot players who are about to leave and bring them back with offers that match what they actually want.
Tech stack:
Snowflake (with Snowpipe and Cortex Analyst LLM agent).
dbt, Python.
Airflow (Astronomer), AWS (S3, Lambda), Terraform, GitHub Actions.
Omni BI on top. Reverse ETL into marketing platforms.
Medallion architecture: bronze, silver, gold.
We’re early in the build, and the person we hire helps shape the foundation.
Role Overview
You sit between the business (Product Owner, stakeholders) and the engineering team.

About 30% of your time is practical data work: validating that CDP capabilities behave as specified, exploring data before specs are written, testing hypotheses for new capabilities, building shared gold layer models that consumer teams use, and helping those teams understand what data is available. It is not business reporting or analytics built for individual brands.

The other 70% is Systems Analysis: turning what you find into specs engineers can build from, owning data quality from symptom to root cause, raising what isn’t right before it ships, and shaping the semantic layer so other business lines can find and trust the data.
Four things define the systems analysis side:

Turn business intent into specs that engineers can build from. Talk to stakeholders, figure out what they actually need, and write it down. Senior Data Analyst (UK/EU job market) / System Analyst (could be on CIS job market)


Department: Data Platform

Reports to: Data Engineering Lead

Employment Type: Full Time

Work Model: Fully Remote

Location: Remote EMEA


About Traffic Label

Traffic Label is a fast-growing, data-driven technology company operating in the iGaming and affiliate marketing space, building scalable products focused on performance, analytics, and automation across global markets.



What we’re building

We’re building a Customer Data Platform: one unified player profile across our group of brands. The platform should help the business:


  • Improve acquisition. Understand who actually converts, and why others don’
  • Increase conversion. Replace generic flows with journeys built for the specific player in front of us.
  • Segment players properly. Who’s a high-value player? Who’s at risk of leaving? Who’s a VIP?
  • Reactivate before churn. Spot players who are about to leave and bring them back with offers that match what they actually want.


Tech stack:

  • Snowflake (with Snowpipe and Cortex Analyst LLM agent).
  • dbt, Python.
  • Airflow (Astronomer), AWS (S3, Lambda), Terraform, GitHub Actions.
  • Omni BI on top. Reverse ETL into marketing platforms.
  • Medallion architecture: bronze, silver, gold.

We’re early in the build, and the person we hire helps shape the foundation.


Role Overview

You sit between the business (Product Owner, stakeholders) and the engineering team.

About 30% of your time is practical data work: validating that CDP capabilities behave as specified, exploring data before specs are written, testing hypotheses for new capabilities, building shared gold layer models that consumer teams use, and helping those teams understand what data is available. It is not business reporting or analytics built for individual brands.


The other 70% is Systems Analysis: turning what you find into specs engineers can build from, owning data quality from symptom to root cause, raising what isn’t right before it ships, and shaping the semantic layer so other business lines can find and trust the data.


Four things define the systems analysis side:

  1. Turn business intent into specs that engineers can build from. Talk to stakeholders, figure out what they actually need, and write it down. Each spec covers source schema, target model, transformation logic, business glossary, schedule, data quality checks, consumer, and use case. Stay reachable through development. Engineers will have questions.
  2. Find and fix data quality problems at the root, alongside the engineering function. Trace lineage, identify the cause, work with the data source owner on the fix, check downstream impact, and close the loop.
  3. Confirm what’s delivered does what we said it would. Validate against the spec you wrote. When the data isn’t right, you raise it before it ships.
  4. Make data usable beyond the team that asked for it. Contribute to the semantic layer, data descriptions, and shared definitions so other business lines can find what they need, understand what it means, and trust it without going through you each time.



You work on the Data Platform layer:

CDP build, identity resolution, data quality, semantic definitions, and governance. Business analytics on top of the platform, such as dashboards and performance reports for specific brands, is produced by analyst teams aligned to those brands. Your job is to make sure those teams can do theirs: trustworthy data, clear definitions, fast issue resolution.


The team

You sit within the Data Platform alongside:

  • A Product Owner who sets the roadmap and business priorities.
  • The Data Engineering function. Your manager (Data Engineering Lead) and the engineers building the platform.
  • Embedded QA function. Owns automated validation.
  • Shared DevOps across the wider organisation.


Where you’ll start

Identity resolution. Deterministic matching is already running in our MDM across five data sources. You extend coverage to the remaining sources and validate match quality at scale. The harder cases are future scope: hashed identifiers without keys, duplicates across sources, device and behaviour signals. They start when we add dedicated ML engineering capacity, with no committed timeline yet. You’ll own the spec and acceptance for that work when it begins.

4406038656,https://be.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-in4matic-4406038656?position=24&pageNum=0&refId=KLE0ZoS55zXfqUQ5cxz0iQ%3D%3D&trackingId=uq2rwhMS3iUWgmTDSWBt6Q%3D%3D,Data Analyst,In4Matic,"Brussels, Brussels Region, Belgium","We're looking for a consultant to join our client's team, taking on a pivotal role in implementing and maintaining high-quality data solutions for anti-money laundering (AML) processes. This position is essential for ensuring compliance, efficiency, and scalability across various operations within the organization. You will bridge the gap between data providers and data consumers, aligning technical solutions with business needs while adhering to strict data governance and quality standards.
Responsibilities
Your core responsibilities will encompass various facets of data management:
Stakeholder Alignment & Communication
Facilitate collaboration among IT, Data Hub, Compliance, and Operations to ensure consistent alignment through structured documentation, workshops, and transparent communication.
Convert complex business requirements into actionable data solutions while balancing technical feasibility with regulatory and operational needs.
Data Governance & Quality
Develop and enforce data governance frameworks, including role definitions and quality rules, in conjunction with data providers and consumers.
Proactively monitor and refine data quality issues to maintain the integrity of AML-related datasets.
Document business and technical rules for data integration to ensure compliance and traceability.
Data Analysis & Troubleshooting
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies, particularly in raw data formats.
Conduct analysis of AML Transaction Monitoring data to identify patterns and gaps affecting compliance and operational efficiency.
Role and Profile
The ideal candidate will possess a blend of technical and business expertise, including:
Technical Experience
5+ years of experience in Data Analytics, particularly within AML processes.
Hands-on proficiency in SQL and data modeling tools.
Experience with reporting and dashboard tools.
Business Expertise
Comprehensive understanding of banking data, especially AML Transaction Monitoring.
Ability to capture and manage functional and non-functional requirements for data solutions.
Knowledge of data security and IT architecture within financial services.
Soft Skills And Language Requirements
The successful candidate will demonstrate strong interpersonal skills and a proactive mindset:
Excellent communication skills in Dutch, French, and English to engage with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Proactive, analytical, and problem-solving abilities, with extreme attention to detail and a focus on quality.
Adaptability and a willingness to learn, effectively linking strategic objectives to operational execution.

A Master’s degree in a quantitative discipline is preferred, though candidates with relevant analytics knowledge in other fields will also be considered. The role will involve a hybrid working model, balancing onsite and home working equally.
Contactperson & Reference
Reference #: INW25874
Jeroen Van Hoecke

4418159950,https://be.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-h-f-x-at-sibelga-4418159950?position=28&pageNum=0&refId=KLE0ZoS55zXfqUQ5cxz0iQ%3D%3D&trackingId=Vl52QNYuaodQdXX8jfPStQ%3D%3D,DATA ANALYST (H/F/X),Sibelga,"Brussels, Brussels Region, Belgium","Titre de la fonction
DATA ANALYST (H/F/X)
Type de contrat
CDI
Régime de travail
Temps plein
TRAVAILLER CHEZ SIBELGA, ÇA BOOSTE MON ÉNERGIE !

Sibelga est le gestionnaire du réseau de distribution d'électricité et de gaz naturel dans la Région de Bruxelles-Capitale. Nous assurons un approvisionnement énergétique fiable et durable à nos clients. Nous visons l'innovation et l'efficacité dans nos services.
QUEL SERVICE REJOIGNEZ VOUS?

Digital Business Solutions (DBS), le département informatique de Sibelga, soutient l’ensemble des activités « métier » de Sibelga. Il leur permet de se dérouler efficacement et de se développer. Il veille à doter Sibelga d’un système informatique autonome, aligné sur l’évolution de ses besoins et des technologies.

L’équipe « Data Management & Analytics » développe et maintient une plateforme de données (incluant un datawarehouse) dont l’objectif est de produire de l'information à valeur ajoutée destinée à soutenir la prise de décisions, le pilotage des activités et la gestion de la performance, ainsi qu'à alimenter le reporting officiel.
VOTRE JOB
En tant que Data Analyst, vous êtes responsable des tâches suivantes :
Vous recueillez et comprenez les besoins en reporting managérial et opérationnel exprimés par le business et les Business Process Analystes (BPA).
Vous rédigez et structurez les spécifications business et les analyses fonctionnelles (business rules) en collaboration avec le BPA et les IT Analystes.
Vous prototypez les business rules pour s’assurer de leur validité en SAS Enterprise Guide.
Vous analysez et profilez les données issues des systèmes opérationnels pour valider la qualité de ces dernières.
Vous définissez avec les BPA un glossaire permettant d’aligner l’ensemble des utilisateurs sur les concepts et leur définition utilisés en BI.
Vous modélisez logiquement le modèle d’entreprise DWH et le modèle BI (DataMart).
Vous contribuez à la mise en place des solutions de reporting en SAS VA.
Vous testez et documentez les solutions mises en œuvre en amont des Business Acceptance Tests (BAT).
Vous supportez le business dans la rédaction des tests scenarios (sur base des rapports et des business rules), la définition des test cases et l’exécution des BAT.
Vous définissez avec le business les droits et accès standards accordés aux utilisateurs (en fonction de leur rôle/profil).
Vous préparez les supports de formation (en collaboration avec le BPA) et assurez la formation des utilisateurs finaux.
Vous êtes le premier garant de la qualité des livrables dont vous avez la responsabilité ou sur lesquels vous êtes impliqués :
FDD
Source-to-Target Mapping
EDWH and BI Logical Data Model
SAS VA Reports
Tests & Issue Log
Training material

Vous supportez le Project Manager et le Lead Fonctionnel dans leur prise de décision concernant la conduite du projet, les risques identifiés et les solutions pour les mitiger, le scope à développer et l’effort nécessaire à l’exécution des tâches.
VOTRE PROFIL
Formation : Vous avez un master à orientation économique, IT ou statistique.
Expérience : Vous avez une expérience de minimum 5 ans en Business Intelligence et Datawarehouse dans un rôle d’analyste ou de business analyst.
Compétences linguistiques : maîtrise du français ou du néerlandais, avec une bonne connaissance de la deuxième langue ainsi que de l'anglais.

Compétences techniques : maîtrise des Utilities et/ou une expérience réussie dans le domaine d’au moins 3 ans (idéalement en BI), connaissance de SQL (dans le cadre du prototyping nous utilisons l’outil EGuide), connaissance en SAS VA.

Compétences et attitudes clés : orienté résultat & client, communication, forte résistance au stress, esprit analytique, autonome, capacité d’apprentissage, structuré & organisé pour tenir les délais.
NOTRE OFFRE
Une entreprise certifiée Top Employer, valorisant le bien-être et le développement de ses collaborateurs.
Un job porteur de sens, au service de la ville de Bruxelles et de ses habitants, dans un secteur d’activité en perpétuelle évolution.
Une formation de qualité et valorisante, ainsi que des perspectives de carrière.

Un package financier et extralégal avantageux (possibilités de télétravail, voiture de société électrique avec une carte de chargement ou un budget mobilité, GSM, frais internet, allocation de représentation, 33 jours de congé, chèques-repas, écochèques, flex income plan, assurances telles que soins de santé, hospitalisation et pension, 30% de réduction sur la facture d’énergie, …).
Localisation du poste
Localisation du poste
Quai des Usines 16, 1000 Bruxelles

4409918501,https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/view/aws-data-analyst-data-analyst-at-europa-search-4409918501?position=29&pageNum=0&refId=KLE0ZoS55zXfqUQ5cxz0iQ%3D%3D&trackingId=rK0n7M3XYlbRjnZQsGYQjA%3D%3D,AWS Data Analyst / Data Analyst,Europa Search,European Union,"CANDIDATES MUST BE EU BASED
What you will do
In your day-to-day work, you will:
• Analyse and validate data to ensure accuracy and quality
• Translate business needs into clear data definitions and requirements
• Work closely with stakeholders to understand how data is used and what is
needed
• Identify and resolve data quality issues, including root cause analysis
• Support Data Engineers with data insights, clarifications, and requirements
• Contribute to solution design from a data perspective
• Document data structures, mappings, and definitions in a clear and simple
way
• Support testing and validation of data deliveries
• Write SQL queries and support data transformations
• Make smaller adjustments in data pipelines when needed
What we are looking for
We believe you have:
• Solid experience working with data analysis and large datasets
• Strong SQL skills
• Experience from modern data platforms, preferably AWS
• Good understanding of data modelling and Data Warehousing
• Ability to turn business needs into practical data solutions
• Experience working in agile teams
It is a plus if you also have:
• Experience with AWS tools such as S3, Glue, Athena or Redshift
• Understanding of data pipelines and data engineering practices
• Experience from banking or financial services
Who you are
You are someone who:
• Enjoys working with data and understanding how it creates value
• Is curious, structured, and hands-on
• Likes to work together with others and share knowledge
• Takes ownership and focuses on getting things done
• Is comfortable working in a changing environment

4410578615,https://be.linkedin.com/jobs/view/business-data-analyst-at-teals-consulting-4410578615?position=31&pageNum=0&refId=KLE0ZoS55zXfqUQ5cxz0iQ%3D%3D&trackingId=HClvE%2BilmRLKQBLV9ObPzA%3D%3D,Business Data Analyst,Teals Consulting,Brussels Metropolitan Area,"Teals Consulting is een gespecialiseerd adviesbureau met een vijftigtal medewerkers, actief in de financiele, energie en publieke dienstverlening sectoren.
Actief in België, Frankrijk en Duitsland begeleiden wij onze klanten bij hun transformatieprojecten, met sterke expertise in Data Management, Business Analyse en Analytics.
Momenteel zijn wij op zoek naar een Business Data Analyst (werknemer, geen freelance) ter versterking van ons Smart Data Management team.
Uw rol
Als consultant bij Teals werk je mee aan projecten rond data-exploitatie, met een dubbele focus:
Analyse van businessbehoeften en het formaliseren van bijbehorende processen
Data-analyse, structurering en documentatie van business rules.
Afhankelijk van uw ervaring zal u:
Functionele behoeften rond data-exploitatie begrijpen
Meewerken aan het modelleren van datastromen en het in kaart brengen van businessprocessen (UML, BPMN)
Bijdragen aan het verbeteren van de kwaliteit en betrouwbaarheid van data gebruikt in rapportering en sturingstools
Dataverwerkingen, business rules, filters en transformaties documenteren
Diepgaande analyses uitvoeren met behulp van Python
Visualisaties en analyses creëren in tools zoals Power BI
Samenwerken met IT-, data- en business-teams in een continue verbeteringscontext.
Uw profiel
2 tot 5 jaar ervaring als Data Analyst, Business Analyst of Data Consultant binnen grotere organisaties of een consultancy-omgeving
Sterke data mindset (datakwaliteit, governance, business rules, documentatie)
Ervaring met het analyseren van datastromen en businessprocessen
Goede kennis van SQL en Power BI, en ervaring met data-analyse in Python
Kennis van of ervaring met UML- en BPMN-modellering
Nauwkeurig, gestructureerd en in staat om analyses helder te formuleren
Comfortabel in omgevingen met zowel technische als business-uitdagingen
Goede kennis van het Frans en het Nederlands (C2 in één van de 2 talen & B2 in de andere taal)
Ervaring in gereguleerde omgevingen of met grote datavolumes is een plus.
Ons aanbod
Gevarieerde opdrachten met persoonlijke begeleiding in uw professionele groei
Een stimulerende, mensgerichte werkomgeving met een sterke samenwerkingscultuur

Een aantrekkelijk verloningspakket, inclusief: competitief salaris volgens uw ervaring en skills, collectieve bonus (CCT90), maandelijkse onkostenvergoeding, bedrijfswagen met tankkaart of mobiliteitsbudget, hospitalisatieverzekering, maaltijdcheques, groepsverzekering
Sterke focus op work-life balance.
