The only way we can tackle the challenges of this fast-changing world is with passionate people. Led by our purpose of building trust and solving important problems, we come together at PwC to address the biggest issues facing the world in a way that drives positive impact. Are you eager to join a team fuelled by open and curious minds? People who are passionate about their work and create new solutions for a new day? Then you’re at the right place.
Bring your talent, learn new skills, make a positive impact

Managed Services is the operational execution engine of PwC Advisory. Our mission is to help clients solve complex operational challenges and reach ambitious business objectives. We work hand-in-hand with our colleagues from other PwC Advisory teams to develop, deploy and run operational solutions combining the best experience, methodologies and technologies. Our team turns ideas and insights into concrete actions and results. Under the expert stewardship of our Advisory experts, you’ll delve into the inner workings of companies to find the best solutions for any challenge they might have.

Curious to see how we help our clients transform and move forward in the new industrial and digital age? Wonder what happens behind the scenes of the tailored solutions we design for clients? Whatever your background, if you’re looking for an interesting new challenge, we want to meet you!

In this role, you will work closely with our App & Data team to provide insights and recommendations to drive business growth and improve decision-making processes. You will have the opportunity to work with various data analysis tools, including PowerBI, PowerApps, Excel or other data tools and contribute to business governance and strategy initiatives.
Your Impact:
Collaborate with the team to gather and analyze data from various sources.
Develop and maintain data visualizations and reports using PowerBI and other relevant tools.
Assist in the implementation and maintenance of Custom Tools, PowerApps or Dashboards.
Conduct data analysis to identify trends, patterns and insights to support business decision-making.
Support the development and execution of Data Strategies based on data-driven insights.
Assist in the identification and implementation of process improvements to enhance data quality and accuracy.
About you?
Master or bachelor student in a business study field with interest in data
Willingness to work with the latest tools such as PowerBI, PowerApps and other relevant software.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex data sets in Excel or other tools.
Excellent attention to detail and the ability to work with large datasets.
Interest in business governance principles and strategies.
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, to effectively present findings and recommendations.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
Familiarity with data visualization best practices
Basic understanding of statistical concepts and techniques.
Fluent in English and at least one of the national languages
Proactive, showing initiative and strong organizational skills
Uphold the firm's code of ethics and business conduct
Life at PwC Belgium
Our culture and workplace are something we’re proud of. Here’s a sneak peek at some of the benefits you can look forward to.
Flexibility: flexible working hours so you can decide your most effective working pattern.
Infinite learning and growing: broad opportunities to learn every day and exciting career pathways that help you explore different disciplines or areas of expertise.
Rewards that matter to you: a flexible rewards plan gives you access to a wide range of benefits so you can make the choices that best fit your personal circumstances and lifestyle.

Diversity & inclusion: we believe that diversity makes PwC stronger. It helps us solve important problems and deliver added value to our clients. We encourage an inclusive culture where people can be the best versions of themselves. We organise various D&I initiatives, including an active employee-led Employee Resource Group (ERG) community, comprising 9 diversity groups with over 200 PwC employees aiming to make an impact within and beyond PwC.
A sustainable mobility offer: we offer a comprehensive mobility budget with a wide range of options (hybrid and electric cars, bike leasing, mobility card or loan reimbursement).

Well-being: an extensive health and well-being programme that includes access to professional sport coaching, individual sessions, sports communities and mental health resources. We encourage a healthy lifestyle with regular wellness challenges and various catering options at our cafeteria.
Team events such as Last Friday Drinks, quarterly themed parties, team-building days and celebrations, as well as time spent on community days and charity activities.
International PwC network: broad access to industry expertise, both locally and through our wider global network of member firms.
Supporting you in every phase of your life, including family friendly work schemes.
